title: notification.requested
description: A service asks the notifications module to deliver a notification. Emitted by stapel_core.notifications.request_notification (topic stapel.notifications.requested). Validation is split across the seam: this edge (stapel-core) validates payload SHAPE only — notification_type is a non-empty string, content_html/content_text are strings when present — because core cannot import the notifications type registry; whether notification_type is actually registered is validated notifications-side, in the consumer and by `manage.py check_notifications`. Calls carrying content_html/content_text are the raw-content escape hatch: they may use an unregistered type (group defaults to 'system') and are rendered inside the base brand layout instead of a registered per-type template.
